title: [security] non-constant-time prk_4e3m zero-check in Edhoc*::export_oscore
description: In rust/lichen-oscore/src/edhoc.rs:884 and 1338 (from e98111628), `if !self.state.completed || self.state.prk_4e3m.iter().all(|&b| b == 0)` uses short-circuiting all() which has data-dependent runtime (early exit on first non-zero). Violates constant-time discipline for key-state checks before OSCORE key export. Alternative: use constant-time all-zero test (e.g. from subtle or manual xor-fold). Also affects key handling post-EDHOC. See focus on crypto timing, OSCORE, key handling.
close_reason: Replaced short-circuiting all() with constant-time fold-or for prk_4e3m zero check in both EdhocInitiator and EdhocResponder::export_oscore. Verified with cargo check.
